name: Medical Emergency Response
description: Protocols for campus and field medical emergencies, triage procedures, and first aid guidelines.

documents.title: Campus Medical Emergency Response
documents.content:
CAMPUS MEDICAL EMERGENCY RESPONSE PROTOCOL

1. SCENE SAFETY
- Assess scene for ongoing hazards before approach
- Ensure PPE is available and donned
- Establish safe perimeter around patient
- Remove bystanders from immediate area

2. PRIMARY ASSESSMENT (ABCDE)
- Airway: Check for obstructions, perform head-tilt chin-lift
- Breathing: Look, listen, feel. Check respiratory rate and quality
- Circulation: Check pulse (carotid, radial), skin color, capillary refill
- Disability: AVPU scale (Alert, Voice, Pain, Unresponsive)
- Exposure: Full body check for injuries, maintain body temperature

3. TRIAGE (START System)
- GREEN (Minor): Walking wounded, can wait
- YELLOW (Delayed): Serious but not immediately life-threatening
- RED (Immediate): Life-threatening, requires immediate intervention
- BLACK (Deceased/Expectant): Non-survivable injuries

4. EMERGENCY CONTACTS
- Campus Security: ext. 5555
- Local EMS: 911
- Poison Control: 1-800-222-1222
- Campus Health Center: ext. 2200

5. DOCUMENTATION
- Record time of patient contact
- Document vital signs every 5 minutes
- Note all interventions performed
- Transfer care to EMS with verbal and written report
